methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.8B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Retro Biosciences is a Series A biotech company founded by Joe Betts-LaCroix that has raised $180M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public equities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Chai Discovery, and Asimov.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-Progression uses the $180M total raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding $3.6B. Peer-Median anchors to 7 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables, which exhibit valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B with a median of $1.3B. Public-Comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.7x across the three public tickers) is not applicable; target revenue is not disclosed, limiting use of this method.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $4.1B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around stage-specific step-up assumptions and the dispersion within the peer set. Key sensitivities include the actual capital deployment trajectory post-Series A and whether the company's clinical progress justifies positioning at the upper quartile of peer valuations. Revenue disclosure would narrow confidence in market-rate multiples.
